title: greigia stenolepis
intro: Greigia stenolepis, also known as the "False Agave" or "Puya," is a type of terrestrial bromeliad plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues, skin irritations, and as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Greigia stenolepis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of potential health benefits.
